title: Figure AI Founded
description: Brett Adcock founded Figure AI with the goal of developing general-purpose humanoid robots to address labor shortages. The company is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California.

title: Partnership Agreement Signed with BMW
description: Figure AI announced a partnership with BMW Manufacturing to deploy humanoid robots for trials at BMW's South Carolina plant in the United States, performing tasks that are dangerous for humans.

title: Completed $67.5 Million Series A Funding
description: Figure AI announced the completion of a $67.5 million Series A funding round led by Parkway Venture Capital, aimed at accelerating humanoid robot R&D and corporate expansion.

title: Partnership Agreement Signed with OpenAI
description: Figure AI established a partnership with OpenAI, which will provide large language model technology to help develop humanoid robots capable of understanding natural language instructions.

title: Secured $80 Million in Additional Funding
description: Figure AI completed a new $80 million funding round with investors including Parkway Venture Capital and Intel Capital, bringing total funding to $148 million.

title: Figure 01 Humanoid Robot Unveiled
description: Figure AI officially unveiled its first general-purpose humanoid robot, Figure 01, capable of walking, picking up objects, and performing factory tasks. It stands 5'6" tall and weighs 132 pounds.

title: Figure 01 Showcased at the White House
description: Figure AI participated in an AI summit hosted by the White House, showcasing Figure 01's capabilities. Demonstrations showed the robot learning to make coffee and perform household tasks.

title: Figure 01 Field Tested at BMW Factory
description: Figure 01 officially entered the BMW Spartanburg plant for real-world production testing, performing tasks such as parts selection, machine operation, and material transport.

title: Deployment Testing in Logistics Warehouses
description: Figure AI announced the deployment of Figure 01 at a major logistics company, where the robot began performing box handling and sorting tasks in the warehouse.

title: Figure 02 Released
description: Figure AI released the second-generation humanoid robot, Figure 02, featuring a redesigned hardware architecture optimized for industrial environments and applied to tasks such as vehicle assembly.

title: Terminated Partnership with OpenAI
description: Figure AI CEO Brett Adcock announced the termination of the partnership with OpenAI, deciding to develop internal AI models independently, believing that end-to-end robot control requires deeper vertical integration.

title: Figure Showcased at CES 2025
description: Figure AI showcased next-generation robot technology at CES 2025, demonstrating application potential in home and commercial environments and attracting significant attention.

title: Helix VLA Model Released
description: Figure AI released the Helix vision-language-action model, enabling end-to-end control of general-purpose humanoid robots. Complex tasks can be executed using only natural language instructions, without specific programming.

title: Figure 03 Released
description: Figure AI launched the third-generation humanoid robot, Figure 03, equipped with the Helix model, featuring 32 degrees of freedom across its body and significantly enhanced computing power.

title: Secured $1.5 Billion in Major Funding
description: Figure AI announced the completion of a $1.5 billion funding round at a valuation exceeding $10 billion, with major investors including Microsoft and OpenAI participating. The funds will be used to scale up production.
